/thanks.title: Red Cable Studio
p: Response times typically occur in 1-2 business days.

/about.title: Red Cable Studio
p: I'm drawn to signals. The subtle resonance between what’s being made and what moves people, what earns their trust, and what makes them stop and feel something.

My path has always been at the intersection of design, storytelling, technology, and human connection. I started in music and art, learning how to listen closely, build from scratch, and create with intention. That same instinct drives my work today.

I help teams turn early ideas into products people use, share, and remember. I do my best work in the messy middle, where vision meets real-world constraints. When ideas need focus. When goals need clarity. When the work needs to move forward.

What sets me apart is how I connect strategy to craft. I help tell product stories that resonate, design systems that scale, and work hands-on to get the right outcome. Whether with startups, enterprises, or mission-driven teams, I look for partners who value clarity, quality, new frontiers and care.

The best work I’ve been part of has come from teams that feel more like bands than org charts. Everyone brings something different. Everyone listens. And when it clicks, you create something no one could have done alone.
Right now, I am focused on designing tools and systems that help teams create better, work smarter, and deliver experiences that last. Speed is important. But quality is what makes it matter.

/marathon.title: Red Cable Studio
h1: Bridging the Gap Between Patients and Providers
h3: Live Training. Real Feedback. Any Screen.
h4: Behind and in Front of the Platform
Making Healthcare Work Like It Should
p: We redesigned how patients connect, schedule, and message their care teams. The goal was simple. Cut the noise. Bring back real care.
Legacy tools were clunky. Missed appointments. Frustrated users. We built a new experience that actually works for both sides.
We spoke to real patients, providers, and schedulers. Then we rebuilt everything from the ground up. What mattered most? Fast access. Clear answers. Less friction.
Patients want easy, intuitive tools, no manual needed.
All walks of life, juggling work, and health. Wants care that’s easy to book, simple to understand, and responsive when it counts.
Wears too many hats. Needs fast tools, clear info, and more time for what matters—treating patients, not wrestling software.
Wears too many hats. Needs fast tools, clear info, and more time for what matters: treating patients, not wrestling software.
The app had to work in real clinics. So we designed it with speed, clarity, and scale in mind.
AMSO conceptual framework created by Dr. Michael O'Donnell

We stripped away clutter and focused on what matters. For patients, clear next steps. For providers, tools that work. After rounds of testing, we turned complexity into clarity. Simple. Powerful. Ready to use.
Your goals, messages, appointments, and support are all in one place. Simple layout. No fluff. Just action.
Book, reschedule, or cancel in seconds.
Request refills for yourself and your family in seconds. Track prescriptions and manage your meds, all in one place.
We built a tool anyone could use. Scheduling, messaging, and care plans all in one place. Fast to learn, easy to use. Just a few taps and patients stayed informed, providers stayed focused.
When you design for real people, you see it all. The magic is in making it simple and honest. When you do, you don’t just fix workflows. You improve lives.
When you design for real people, you see it all. The magic is in making it simple and honest. When you do, you don’t just fix workflows. You improve lives..
Patients track key health stats in real time.
Providers get the insights they need to make better calls.

/adobe.title: Red Cable Studio
h1: How Lightroom Made Learning a Superpower
h4: Designing Through  a Lens For Every Photographer
Getting Answers, Right When You Need Them
p: Can we guide users from “where do I start” to “I’ve got this”?

Beginners were overwhelmed, and even experienced users felt lost in its feature maze. The mission? Simplify the journey. Make Lightroom approachable, teachable, and indispensable for every photographer.
User interviews, analytics, and usability tests revealed key pain points. These insights shaped every decision, from simplifying the process to building confidence through non-destructive editing.
Too many tools made Lightroom intimidating.
Users learn differently: some are hands-on, others prefer tutorials.
Fear of breaking edits blocked progress.
Total beginners. They wanted simple, easy-to-use tools to get started without feeling lost.
Comfortable with basics, hungry for more. They needed powerful features made dead simple.
Fast, focused, and no-nonsense. They wanted smooth workflows that didn’t slow them down.
Turning Lightroom into an intuitive journey step by step.
Say what you need. Get what works.
Hands-on lessons built into the app taught users as they worked, step by step.
Animated hints explained tools in seconds, making learning fast and seamless.
Contextual help's tailored tips pop up as you work, giving you the exact help  you need without breaking your flow.
Quick-access controls let you crank up the challenge on the fly, dialing in just the right burn.
Stay up to date with Lightroom's newest features.
Bite-sized lessons that teach you everything from perfect lighting to pro-level effects. Learn fast, apply faster.
Type, search, and instantly pull up the tools, features, or tutorials you need. No scrolling, no guesswork—just results.
From presets to white balance, unlock the full potential of Lightroom’s editing features.
Edit your own photos while following simple, step-by-step guidance. Hands-on learning, made for you.
Real-time lessons, right inside your workflow. Learn as you create.
Step-by-step in-app tutorials right where you need them, inside the app. Master tools and techniques while working on your photos in real time.
Get instant, interactive guidance with visuals that show you how to master features and speed up your workflow. No fluff, just results.
Lightroom wasn’t just a creative tool anymore, it was a teacher, empowering creativity at every level.
16.7M views, 8M unique users in a year.
Simplicity isn’t simple, it’s strategic. Teaching users requires empathy, clear guidance, and a focus on real-world application.
Behavioral driven learning paths, advanced tutorial tracks, and applying this approach to other Adobe tools can take this success even further.

/luster.title: Red Cable Studio
h1: Every Moment Creating the Big Picture
p: From capture to print, I designed a system that turned live events into lasting stories. Fast. Simple. Built for teams that don’t have time to waste.
How do we cut the drag and give teams the power to deliver the moment while it’s still fresh?
Crowds showed up. Magic happened. Then the photos sat in Dropbox for weeks. Artemis had a product. What it needed was a full pipeline—from booth to print to memory—in minutes, not months.
They want their moment fast, seamless, and unforgettable. No waiting, just pure “Wow!”
The experience orchestrators making the magic happen. They need tools that work as fast as they do.
We dug into the process. Interviewed the team. Watched the workflow. Then we mapped out the real blockers.
It took too long to go from camera to print. Excitement fizzled.
The team juggled cameras, spreadsheets, and separate tools.
No clear way to track progress or fix bottlenecks on the fly.
We built the Mosaic Manager App to make every step smooth. One tool to run the wall from start to finish.
The dashboard puts brand reps in command. They see every photo, control every step, and protect the mosaic’s quality on the spot.
Keep the Good. Toss the Rest.
Moderation tools make it easy to approve the best photos and reject the ones that miss the mark.
The Print Manager gives reps control over queues, reprints, and approvals. Every print, flawless and on time.
Stay on Track. Hit the Goal.
Milestones help teams set daily targets and keep the mosaic reveal running on schedule.
From 45 minutes to under 5. Guests stayed engaged and excited throughout the event.
Teams moved 2x faster and knew exactly what to own.
Return rates improved, driven by clearer communication and better flow.
Speed wins. We simplified until there was nothing left to slow them down.
Speed wins. We simplified until there was nothing left to slow them down..
One tool is better than five. Centralized ops turned the tide.
Expansion to live streaming events•  Automated photo tagging•  Automated filters for guest expression detection
Computer vision guest expression detection. Photo tag automation

/archive/forme.title: Ray Canapini - Portfolio
p: I led the design for FORME Life’s flagship Live 1:1 personal training product, merging proprietary hardware and mobile into one fluid experience. The platform combined live streaming, remote Studio control, biometrics, and personalized workouts—transforming personal training with real-time feedback and data-driven results.

How do we create a seamless, powerful connection between clients and trainers—live, on any device or studio hardware? The goal: make every session effortless to start, smooth to run, and packed with results. It’s not just about bridging the gap; it’s about making that connection so strong, they’ll never feel the distance.
Busy executives who value fitness need workouts that fit their schedule, not the other way around.
Tight schedule, irregular hours, need for flexible yet effective sessions.
Top-tier trainer with a packed schedule, limited by location—ready to grow without sacrificing quality.
Location-bound, limited capacity, needs a way to scale without sacrificing quality.
Identifying clients and trainers essential needs. Through interviews, surveys, and usability tests, we found out exactly what would make this platform essential.
Wanted real-time, live interaction with trainers, preferring it over pre-recorded sessions.
Wanted seamless, cross-device navigation for session control and progress stats.
Clients and trainers struggled with clunky tools—so we simplified control, stats, and feedback.

The original Trainer App MVP came with gaps—limited functionality, a custom design that didn’t scale. We needed a flexible, scalable UI. I rallied key stakeholders, led a native iOS redesign, and cut design and development time by 30%. The result? A streamlined platform that met client and trainer needs—fast and frictionless.
Own your calendar—see, edit, and plan ahead in a snap.
Talk straight to your clients, swap videos, and guide them on the fly.
All your clients, one list—no hunting, no hassle.
See who’s on deck and jump right into live training.
Raw stats and metrics to shape their next big win.
Craft killer workouts, tweak every set and rep.

This is the heart of your trainer app—sweat meets software in real-time. Your client’s front and center, pushing hard as you fine-tune every move and crank up the burn. Digital and physical combine into one unified space.
Quick-access controls let you crank up the challenge on the fly, dialing in just the right burn.
Guiding clients through every moment to ensure perfect form and maximum gains.
Your client is right there, live and in action,  pushing through every rep.
Trainers take control with ease—see the client’s grind, track their burn, and fine-tune their effort in real time.

Delivered a fully integrated platform allowing users to access real-time, one-on-one training sessions. Key features included seamless session booking, in-app notifications, and adaptive feedback mechanisms. This UX overhaul led to:

I created and led “The Guild,” a cross-functional group of 12+ key stakeholders from product, engineering, personal training, and business. The wins? Aligned voices and vision, a unified platform, and focused goals kept on track.
30% faster time-to-market for new features on iOS
Higher user engagement with longer session times and increased return rates
Successful launch directly supported the $69.9M fundraising round, showcasing the business and user value of the platform
Testing every detail made the final product indispensable.
Early collaboration kept everyone on target—and it showed.
AI-driven plans and real-time feedback for maximum results.
Localized content and multilingual support to serve clients everywhere.

/forme.title: Ray Canapini - Portfolio
h3: Live Training. Real Feedback. Any Screen.
h4: Designing for Space, Light and Reflection
Trainer App: Real-Time Coaching, Lasting Gains
Built for Speed. Tuned for Impact.
h5: Clarity. Control. Confidence. One Frame at a Time.
p: I led design for FORME Life’s flagship 1:1 personal training platform. We combined connected hardware, mobile, and live video into one seamless flow. Trainers could guide every move, control studio hardware settings remotely, and respond in real time with biometric feedback. The result was personal training without the limits.
How do you design for a connection that’s felt, not seen — where the screen fades and the goal takes over?

You make the connection fast, fluid, and strong enough to feel personal. I designed a cross-device experience that starts in seconds, runs without friction, and delivers real results. No gaps. No guesswork. Just real-time coaching that feels close, even when you’re miles apart.

We sat down with real clients and trainers. We asked, we listened, and we tested. Interviews, surveys, and hands-on sessions revealed what they actually needed, not nice-to-haves, but mission-critical features that made the platform feel indispensable.

The first Trainer App was broken. Limited tools. Clunky, inconsistent, and stuck. Clients wanted real connection. Trainers needed simple control.  I pivoted with a full redesign built on iOS standards and components.

Product and engineering aligned fast. We shipped quickly, cut delivery time by 30%, and delivered what both sides needed. A platform that just worked.  But speed was not enough. We had to get it right.  I worked directly with trainers to map workflows, pressure-test designs, and cut anything that slowed them down. Every screen and control was designed to perform live, in real sessions. Simple to use. Powerful when it matters.
Chose real-time coaching over pre-recorded videos. They wanted a human, not a playlist.
Needed smoother controls across devices to stay focused on coaching, not tapping.
Both sides were frustrated. The tools felt clunky. The experience needed to get out of the way.
Top-tier trainer with a packed schedule, limited by location, ready to grow without sacrificing quality.
Location-bound, limited capacity, needs a way to scale without sacrificing quality.
Busy executives who value fitness need workouts that fit their schedule, not the other way around.
Tight schedule, irregular hours, need for flexible yet effective sessions.

The FORME Studio and Lift are not just screens. They are mirrors with displays hidden behind them. Black reflects. White reveals. Every pixel had to balance function with reflection.  Iconography and typography were the hardest to perfect. We created a high-contrast grayscale system tuned for this unique surface.The goal was simple: erase the barrier between trainer and client and create one shared space.  I collaborated with hardware, engineering, and key trainers to get it right. We tested on real devices, in real sessions, to fine-tune every detail.  The result was a seamless experience where digital and physical felt like one. Sessions looked better, worked better, and felt better for everyone.

This is where it all comes together, real-time training where sweat meets software. Your client’s in motion, live on screen. You control the pace, adjust the intensity, and guide every rep. One seamless space, fully connected. No delay, no distractions, just pure coaching power.
Monitor the move, track stats, and fine-tune every second of effort.
Adjust intensity instantly and dial in the challenge without breaking flow.
Stay visible and hands-on, cueing perfect form and progress.
See your client live and in motion, pushing every rep in real time.
See who’s on deck and jump right into live training.
Own your calendar: see, edit, and plan ahead in a snap.
All your clients, one list. No hunting. No hassle.
Raw stats and metrics to shape their next big win.
Craft killer workouts, tweak every set and rep.
Talk to clients clearly, swap videos fast, guide in real time.
Every session should look and feel premium, not improvised. So I created a set of livestream guides that cover lighting, framing, and setup so every session looks intentional and on point.
Leadership wanted more features, faster. The team wanted clarity, proof, and purpose. I made sure we gave them both.

At the start, everything was siloed. Teams signals were crossed. Business goals were fuzzy. So I brought the right people to the table–product, engineering, and training leads. We called it The Guild.

We met regularly. No fluff. No slides. Just straight talk about what mattered: vision, goals, and progress. We built trust, cut the noise, and kept momentum. The Guild stayed tight and shipped what counted.

At the start, everything was siloed. Teams signals were crossed. Business goals were fuzzy. So I brought the right people to the table–product, engineering, and training leads. We called it, "The Guild."
We met executive velocity goals by streamlining scope, simplifying workflows, and removing blockers. No bloat, just momentum.
We used customer feedback, and session data to inform what shipped. Less guessing. More signal.
Instead of slowing down, we built modular tools and flows that kept pace with the roadmap and stayed grounded in real needs.
By aligning product, design, and execs early, we kept priorities sharp and shipped with fewer detours.
We tested everything, nothing shipped unproven
Able to stay flexible, push back thoughtfully, and deliver on fast-changing leadership goals. Clear on separating business needs, opportunities, and desires to keep teams focused on real value.
Multilingual and localized support on deck
